title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' stars Matt Damon as Odysseus, focusing on human themes over divine intervention.
The film, featuring non-linear narrative and practical effects, departs from Homer's original while retaining spectacle.
Anne Hathaway and Tom Holland play Penelope and Telemachus, exploring family dynamics and Odysseus's moral challenges.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle married his partner Devin during the All-Star break.
The couple celebrated their marriage at Arya Trattoria in Boston's North End.
Tolle, drafted in 2024, has a 5-7 record with a 3.59 ERA over 18 career starts.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' interior defensive line features Milton Williams and Christian Barmore, who combined for 86 pressures last season.
New England's defensive line ranked sixth in rushing defense, allowing 101.7 yards per game, despite lacking edge depth.
Durden and Farmer are set to increase their roles as Patriots address depth after losing Khyiris Tonga to free agency.

title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam, 28, died in a skydiving accident in Orange, Mass., due to a gust of wind.
Gaddam recently obtained his A License in skydiving, indicating he completed at least 25 jumps.
Originally from India, Gaddam graduated from Northeastern University in 2024 and worked as a software developer in Boston.

title: Is the real summer turnaround in Boston sports coming from Legacy FC?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Legacy FC secures its first road win with a 1-0 victory over Orlando Pride.
Boston's Legacy FC rebounds from a 0-5 start, now eyeing NWSL playoffs after recent successes.
Legacy FC to face second-place Washington Spirit this Sunday at Centreville Bank Stadium.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 16, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Nana's Green Tea opens July 17 in Back Bay, offering Japanese matcha drinks and mochi parfaits at 175 Newbury St.
C’Yool Yemeni Café and Bakery opens this July in the South End, featuring Yemeni fare and freshly roasted coffee.
About Thyme Lebanese Street Café opens August 1 in Harvard Square, offering saj-wrapped dishes and labneh toppings.

title: 19-year-old dies in apparent drowning in Merrimack River
author: John Waller
bullets: Robert Batchelder, 19, from Manchester, NH, drowned while swimming in the Merrimack River in Canterbury.
The incident occurred around 1 p.m. Wednesday when Batchelder struggled and went under before help arrived.
Concord firefighters recovered Batchelder, who was pronounced dead at a local hospital; the death is not considered suspicious.

title: Pilot rescued after small plane crashes into Plymouth pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A two-seat plane crashed into Curlew Pond in Myles Standish State Forest, Plymouth, shortly before 8 a.m. Thursday.
The pilot sustained serious injuries and was rescued by the Plymouth County Dive Team, then taken to a hospital.
Authorities are investigating the incident to determine if additional occupants were on board the aircraft.

title: ‘Weird Al’ Yankovic promises to super-size his weirdness at TD Garden Saturday
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: 'Weird Al' Yankovic will perform at TD Garden on Saturday, July 18, as part of his Bigger & Weirder 2026 Tour.
Yankovic's tour features a larger production and band, marking his first arena shows despite not releasing new music since 2014.
He credits his long-lasting success to his band's talent and plans to possibly collaborate with the Boston Pops in the future.

title: The entire Mass. House delegation voted to cut Israel aid. Here’s what they said.
author: The Associated Press and Boston.com Staff
bullets: The entire Massachusetts House delegation voted to cut $3.3 billion in U.S. aid to Israel, citing humanitarian concerns.
The vote on the amendment was 104-314, indicating a shift in attitudes within the Democratic Party regarding Israel's policies.
Massachusetts Representatives expressed support for Israel's defense but stressed the need for conditions on military aid compliance.
